name: fs_account_director_replay
description: Replay of external user session sess_1677bc056421 (run 2026-05-18 on v0.7) through v0.8. Customer turns are her exact messages; agent responses are regenerated by v0.8 with sharpener + tensions + close-out. This is a LOWER BOUND on v0.8 quality — v0.7's probes shaped her answer order, so v0.8 sometimes gets non-sequiturs in reply. Real v0.8 conversation would be at least this coherent.
use_case_seed: FS Account Director agent for CSM team working on Financial Services customers

turns.customer: Hi, we're looking to build an AI agent to support our Financial Services Account Directors
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: we have too many customers and not enough dedicated account directors to support them
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: more about giving each AD better visibility and prep work so they can be more efficient with their time
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: weekly internal account team meetings; weekly meetings with the customer; reactive troubleshooting or chasing down open items (tickets; requests; etc.); searching across slack channels for updates from internal teams (EPD, sales/AE, CX, support)
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: usually some mix - if it's a support ticket, most context is in there but if it is something product related, it should be a linear ticket but the context needed to get back to the customer is usually buried in slack threads that you have to find in the right channel (collab-, int-account, mothership, discuss-)
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: pulling all that context in, confirming with the teams working on it that it is accurate and up-to-date and then making sure all the disconnected parts (slack, tickets, relay, etc) have the same up to date context
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: checking linear, zendesk, slack for who is involved and then pinging those people on slack for updates/confirmation/approval and then passing all that back to the relative linear/zendesk tickets and keeping relay updated
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: could be all of the above

turns.customer: they spend significantly less time context-gathering and manually updating
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: actually let me correct that - 2 hours per account saved per week; directors manage multiple accounts so it adds up

turns.customer: the review and validation should be done in advance of regular scheduled customer meetings. the individual leads should be responsible for validating the context before the AD shares with the customer. the challenge is, there are a number of different atlanians who are involved across different projects or new product rollout
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: no - meaning it's many teams across Atlan - Atlan is the company, Atlanians are the employees

turns.customer: depends on the project

turns.customer: all of those - approving/rejecting proposals, getting pinged for updates, and accessing a dashboard view of account status

turns.customer: updates, blockers, decisions made - tracking overall progress of the program along with areas of opportunity
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: upsell and expansion opportunities - new products, new use cases, new business units or geographies
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: the agent should do industry specific research, along with account specific context to recommend areas to probe for opportunities
turns.tests: (replayed from sess_1677bc056421 — external user trace, originally v0.7)

turns.customer: start with the AD - they'll know who to route it to from there. also, the agent should leverage context from other accounts in the same industry to inform recommendations - what similar customers are doing, common use cases, etc.

turns.customer: industry, product mix, and current use cases - those three factors define account similarity
